summary: User policy for Windows Spotlight features as a whole. Good for a quieter, less promotional Windows experience, but it removes Spotlight-based suggestions and related Microsoft content surfaces.

setting.name: Windows Spotlight Features
setting.casual_explanation: Windows Spotlight powers various suggestions, tips, and promotional surfaces in Windows. This policy decides whether those Spotlight features are allowed for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable Spotlight features to reduce suggestions, promotional content, and Microsoft-delivered recommendations in Windows.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning Spotlight off reduces distractions and promotional content, but it also removes some Microsoft tips and dynamic recommendation surfaces.
setting.professional_notes: The Experience Policy CSP uses the allow-form name AllowWindowsSpotlight, while CloudContent.admx maps the corresponding user Group Policy to HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent\DisableWindowsSpotlightFeatures with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the user policy for Windows Spotlight features.

allowed_values.label: Windows Spotlight features allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and Spotlight features remain available.

allowed_values.label: Windows Spotlight features disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Spotlight features are blocked by policy for the current user.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableWindowsSpotlightFeatures = 1, which matches the documented Group Policy disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The Experience Policy CSP documents the allow-form default as allowed, while the registry policy baseline remains not configured unless the policy is set.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want the normal Windows Spotlight experience
avoid_for: Users who dislike suggestions and promotional content
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its standard Spotlight-driven tips and content behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a quieter interface with fewer recommendations.
tradeoffs: No policy-based restriction is enforced for Spotlight features.

recommended_profiles.label: Quiet UI
intended_for: Users who prefer a cleaner interface
avoid_for: Users who want Microsoft tips and Spotlight-driven experiences

states.rationale: Turn off Windows Spotlight features through Group Policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want fewer Microsoft-delivered recommendations and Spotlight surfaces.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You like Windows Spotlight tips or suggestions.
tradeoffs: Promotional and Spotlight-driven content is reduced.
Some Microsoft tips and related dynamic experiences disappear.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: AllowWindowsSpotlight
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the allow-form policy controlling Windows Spotlight features and the related default behavior.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft CloudContent.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the user Group Policy to DisableWindowsSpotlightFeatures with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the CloudContent policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableWindowsSpotlightFeatures = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Local Microsoft CloudContent policy files document the exact user-policy surface, numeric mapping, and all-up Spotlight behavior. The app writes the documented disabled value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="DisableWindowsSpotlightFeatures" class="User" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent" valueName="DisableWindowsSpotlightFeatures">; CloudContent.adml: If you enable this policy setting, Windows spotlight on lock screen, Windows tips, Microsoft consumer features and other related features will be turned off.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact user-policy key and value name; local ADML confirms that enabling the policy turns off Windows Spotlight features as a whole.
